text.title: Prompt Changelog Discipline — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the prompt-changelog-discipline methodology. Sources: Anthropic prompt-engineering iteration chapter; Microsoft LMOps team writeups on prompt versioning; Vellum / Pezzo / Helicone prompt-management docs.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `spec` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r1).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `spec` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r2).

rationale.source: Microsoft LMOps team writeups on prompt versioning

rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `spec` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r3).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `spec` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r4).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `spec` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r5).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.
